source_citation: Larran et al. 2017, Pest Manag Sci, Table 4 (allelic version C, R1 population, 1/8 clones)
notes: Confirmed directly from paper's own Table 4 - S population (KY781916) explicitly labeled wild-type/susceptible, 8/8 clones identical. Native numbering = Arabidopsis thaliana convention per paper's own text, and matches the 1Z8N crystal structure's own residue numbering exactly (verified: residue 574 in 1Z8N chain A is TRP). No offset/alignment work needed, unlike PPO. Previously-reported TSR substitution (bold in Table 4) - well-established in the ALS literature (Tranel & Wright 2002 review, cited as ref 36).

source_citation: Larran et al. 2017, Pest Manag Sci, Table 4 (allelic version H, R2 population, 2/8 clones)
notes: Same wild-type reference as Trp574Leu. Two independent clones (both accession KY781923.1 after dedup - Table 4 lists frequency 2/8) carry this single, isolated substitution. Verified: residue 653 in 1Z8N chain A is SER. Previously-reported TSR substitution (bold in Table 4).

source_citation: Larran et al. 2017, Pest Manag Sci, Table 4 (allelic version E, R2 population, 2/8 clones); position 122 is an established ALS TSR site (Tranel & Wright 2002)
notes: Larran 2017 reports A122S in A. palmeri (first plant report of Ser at 122; position 122 is a well-established ALS TSR site). CAVEAT: in Larran's deposits A122S co-occurs with A282D (allelic version E / KY781920), so A122S individual causality was not isolated; confidence=medium. Structure mapping: 1Z8N chain A residue 122 is ALA (verified), Arabidopsis-convention numbering, in the dimer-interface active-site core. Additional clean single-allele A. palmeri ALS positions (Pro197, Asp376) require the paywalled Kupper et al. 2018 Pest Manag Sci 74:x (DOI 10.1002/ps.5232) - see HANDOFF_NEXT_STEPS.md.

source_citation: Singh et al. 2018, Pest Manag Sci 74:2286-2295 (Pro197Ala detected in A. palmeri, MIS11-A); Ji et al. 2025, Plants 14:525 (Pro197 in A. palmeri); position 197 is a well-established ALS TSR site (Tranel & Wright 2002)
notes: Singh 2018 detected Pro197Ala in A. palmeri (found together with Trp574Leu; Pro197Ala confers SU resistance per the paper). Reference-numbered to the A. palmeri ALS reference KT833339.1 (susceptible); Singh deposited no per-plant mutant accessions (their 'accessions' are field localities, not GenBank deposits), so no paired mutant accession is claimed - same treatment as the ACCase reference-numbered rows. Pro197 is one of the most firmly established ALS TSR positions across weeds. Structure mapping: 1Z8N chain A residue 197 is PRO (verified), Arabidopsis-convention numbering, in the dimer-interface active-site core. Asp376 was NOT added for A. palmeri: Singh 2018 lists it only as a known locus and did not detect it in their A. palmeri plants, and no A. palmeri Asp376 primary source is in hand. A different, non-A.-palmeri weed source for Asp376Glu was found and added instead - see the Asp376Glu row below.

source_citation: Palma-Bautista C, Vazquez-Garcia JG, Osuna MD, Garcia-Garcia B, Torra J, Portugal J, De Prado R (2022) Frontiers in Plant Science 13:1011596
notes: Real weed-evolved allele: three independent tribenuron-methyl-resistant Sinapis alba (white mustard) field populations, GenBank OP681621 (resistant) vs OP681622 (susceptible). Paper's own text: 'Positions refer to the known ALS gene sequence of Arabidopsis thaliana (X51514)' - the same numbering convention already used for every other row in this table, so no offset/re-alignment work was needed. Verified: 1Z8N chain A residue 376 is ASP (matches expected wild-type). Cleaner than the existing Ala122Ser row: sequencing found no other amino-acid-changing polymorphism anywhere else in the gene (paper's own text: 'Several single-nucleotide polymorphisms were identified, but they did not result in any amino acid substitutions' aside from the causal Asp376Glu SNP), so causality is not confounded by a co-occurring second substitution. Caveat: the paper's own title and abstract state resistance in these populations is due to BOTH this target-site mutation AND enhanced (P450-mediated) metabolism acting together - the TSR component (Asp376Glu) is what is recorded here; the NTSR component is a separate, unquantified contribution and is not itself pooled into this structural dataset.
